title: terminalia gracilis var. macrocarpa
intro: Terminalia gracilis var. macrocarpa is a plant species in the Combretaceae family, commonly known as the large-fruited or Malabar Terminalia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Terminalia gracilis var. macrocarpa include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve its leaves and fruits being used to make decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
